skill.name: enhance-prompt
skill.description: Transforms rough UI ideas into structured, Stitch-ready prompts with design system context, component language, visual direction, and clear page structure.
skill.summary: Enhances vague UI prompts into structured prompts for better Stitch generation results.
skill.author: google-labs-code

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many severe patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline UI tokens, and normal documentation text. The skill contains no executable scripts and no confirmed malicious intent; residual risk is limited to a documentation URL and scoped Read/Write instructions for project prompt files.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences or inline code examples, not executable skill logic. No command execution instruction or script was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain fenced Markdown blocks, inline code tokens, or README examples. They do not define runnable code paths or ask the agent to execute shell commands.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography And Credential Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and Windows SAM database findings are triggered by normal words, headings, UI color examples, and repeated DESIGN.md references. No credential file access, password dumping, hashing workflow, or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is UI prompt writing and design documentation. The cited lines do not reference SAM files, password hashes, or cryptographic algorithms as operational behavior.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The skill points users to the official Stitch prompting guide. This is a benign hardcoded URL, but it means the agent may consult external documentation when following the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is directly present and is used as documentation guidance. It is not paired with credential access, data upload, or hidden exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Scoped Project File Read And Optional Write
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to look for DESIGN.md and optionally write an enhanced prompt file when the user requests it. This is expected for the skill, but users should review file writes before accepting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Read and Write are explicitly declared tools, and the instructions constrain reads to DESIGN.md and writes to prompt output files. No broad filesystem traversal or sensitive path targeting was found.

content.value_statement: Vague UI prompts often produce inconsistent layouts and weak visual direction. This skill turns rough ideas into structured Stitch prompts with design system details, UI terminology, and clear page sections.
actual_capabilities: Expands vague UI requests into specific Stitch-ready prompts.
Adds platform, page type, visual style, color roles, and component details.
Uses DESIGN.md when present to include project design system context.
Structures output with a design system section and numbered page sections.
Suggests targeted prompts for small UI edits without changing unrelated elements.
Can write the enhanced prompt to next-prompt.md or a user-named file when requested.
limitations: It does not generate the final UI directly inside Stitch.
It depends on the quality and accuracy of the user input.
It may infer missing design details when the user does not provide them.
It is focused on UI prompts and is not a general writing assistant.

use_cases.title: Clarify a New Screen Idea
use_cases.description: Turn a short screen concept into a detailed prompt with layout, visual tone, components, and color guidance.

use_cases.title: Improve Poor Generation Results
use_cases.description: Rewrite a weak prompt with stronger structure and more precise UI language before trying another Stitch generation.

use_cases.title: Keep Multi-Screen Designs Consistent
use_cases.description: Use an existing DESIGN.md file to make prompts follow shared colors, typography, and component rules.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance a Basic UI Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance this Stitch prompt for better UI generation: "[paste simple UI idea]".

prompt_templates.title: Add Design System Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance this prompt using the project DESIGN.md file. Keep the requested screen focused on [goal] and include the relevant design system details.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Targeted UI Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve one change without broad redesign
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this edit request into a precise Stitch prompt: "[paste edit]". Preserve the existing page and change only the requested area.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Multi-Section Product Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Stitch-ready prompt for a [platform] [page type] for [audience]. Include visual style, design tokens, component hierarchy, states, and section-by-section layout.

output_examples.output: A clean login page prompt with a centered card, email and password fields, a primary sign-in button, recovery link, footer link, and a light professional design system.
A focused edit prompt that places a search input in the header, defines its icon, placeholder, size, focus behavior, and states that existing layout should remain unchanged.

output_examples.input: make a dashboard for my fitness app
output_examples.output: A structured dashboard prompt with mobile-first layout, progress cards, activity charts, goal summaries, navigation, and energetic health-focused visual styling.

best_practices: Provide the target platform, audience, and page purpose before asking for enhancement.
Keep DESIGN.md current so generated prompts use the correct brand and component rules.
Ask for one focused edit at a time when refining an existing screen.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad redesigns when you only need one small UI change.
Do not remove important product constraints from the original prompt.
Do not accept inferred colors or style choices without checking them against your brand.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It rewrites rough UI ideas into detailed Stitch prompts with structure, design language, and visual direction.

faq.question: Does it create the final interface?
faq.answer: No. It creates a stronger prompt that you can use with Stitch or another UI generation workflow.

faq.question: Can it use my design system?
faq.answer: Yes. It looks for DESIGN.md and can include relevant colors, typography, and component guidance.

faq.question: Can it write files?
faq.answer: Yes, when requested. It can write the enhanced prompt to next-prompt.md or another filename you specify.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found no confirmed malicious behavior. The remaining concerns are low risk and relate to documentation access and scoped file operations.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
